db_schema_note: pokemon_flavor_text uses game_version TEXT slug (e.g. 'sun', 'legends-arceus'), NOT integer version_id. All entries below use the correct slug.
legends_za_note: 'legends-za' added to GAME_GENERATION_MAP in FlavorTextSection.tsx (Generation IX) on 2026-07-21. All Legends Z-A entries are now importable. See PHASE_5_DATA_GAP_SPEC.md §7 for full reconciliation notes.

review:text_contains_name_reference.note: Some entries contain 'hodad' — a local Hawaiian slang term. This IS canonical Pokédex text; no action needed, just flagged for awareness.
Entries reference 'Keokeo' (older Alolan name for Vulpix). Canonical text; no action needed.
Entries reference deity incarnate lore. Canonical text; no action needed.

entries.flavor_text: It has a terrible personality, but its saliva and feces are used as ingredients in herbal medicine.
It is nocturnal and is very active at nighttime. But while the sun is up, it tucks itself away and sleeps.
At dusk, these Pokémon appear in great numbers. They battle for territory, biting and scratching each other.
It lurks in the darkness and moves about under cover of night. It naps during the day, using Yungoos as a lookout.
It only moves around at night. It gnaws on whatever it can find, not caring if it's trash or if it belongs to someone.
This form of Rattata was originally brought to Alola to deal with the Yungoos that had been imported to help control the Rattata population.
It has a terrible personality, but its saliva and feces are used as ingredients in herbal medicine.
It is nocturnal and is very active at nighttime. But while the sun is up, it tucks itself away and sleeps.
It is the boss Pokémon of a group of Rattata. It commands its minions to gather food for it, for which it is very picky.
It sends Rattata out ahead of itself to gather food. If any of that food doesn't suit Raticate's refined tastes, it will bite the Rattata that gathered it.
It commands a nest of several Rattata. They all answer to this Raticate and live together under its rule.
Even if you defeat a Raticate, it will come back with a large group of Rattata and seek revenge.
It rules over a nest of several Rattata. It has no interest in food gathered by anyone other than its own minions.
Crafty and cautious, this Pokémon won't reveal itself from the nest until it knows exactly what's going on around it.
It is the boss Pokémon of a group of Rattata. It commands its minions to gather food for it, for which it is very picky.
It sends Rattata out ahead of itself to gather food. If any of that food doesn't suit Raticate's refined tastes, it will bite the Rattata that gathered it.
It is a rare Pokémon that is only found in Alola. It is very popular for its cute disposition and its distinctive pancake-shaped tail.
It's not clear why it changed so radically from Raichu's form in other regions. Perhaps the climate or food in Alola made it change.
Raichu's original form has long since been forgotten. It was changed by the energy of the strange waves emanating from the Alola region.
The people of Alola call it Hodad Raichu. Raichu rides the electric current it accumulates in its tail, surfing through the air.
In Alola, Raichu uses psychic power to ride on its own tail while it floats in the sky.
It only lives in Alola, where it uses its psychic power to spin its tail around and float through the air.
Raichu's original form has long since been forgotten. It was changed by the energy of the strange waves emanating from the Alola region.
The people of Alola call it Hodad Raichu. Raichu rides the electric current it accumulates in its tail, surfing through the air.
Lives on snowy mountains. It protects itself from the cold by wrapping its body in ice.
In the cold climes of Mount Lanakila, Sandshrew converted from its sandy form to an icy one.
This Sandshrew came to live on the snowy peak of a volcano after losing its battle for territory on the ground.
Its steel-hard body endures the cold mountain air without a shiver. It descends to lower elevations in search of food.
This Sandshrew was forced to adapt to cold lands where there is no sand. Its body transformed into ice over many generations.
When this Pokémon rolls itself up into a ball, it protects itself with a sturdy, icy hide that can deflect any blow.
In the cold climes of Mount Lanakila, Sandshrew converted from its sandy form to an icy one.
Its steel-hard body endures the cold mountain air without a shiver. It descends to lower elevations in search of food.
It is adept at climbing trees and rocky cliffs. Its steel claws can cut rock.
Strong winds and cold temperatures have transformed the tips of its claws and spikes into icy blades.
A powerful kick from its hind legs launches it into the air, where it cuts enemies with its steel claws.
The icy spikes on its back are incredibly sharp. People living in snowy mountain areas warn others not to go near them.
The cold has caused its body to become as hard as ice. It attacks with its long, sharp claws.
It has a calm disposition and won't be aggravated by anything. Its ice blades can cut through almost any material.
Strong winds and cold temperatures have transformed the tips of its claws and spikes into icy blades.
The icy spikes on its back are incredibly sharp. People living in snowy mountain areas warn others not to go near them.
It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.
In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.
If you carelessly approach it because it's cute, the boss of the pack, Ninetales, will appear and freeze you.
They live together in a skulk, helping one another. Before eating their prey, they freeze it solid with their –58 degree Fahrenheit breath.
It looks like snow come to life, and the breath it exhales is −58 degrees Fahrenheit. Another name for it is Keokeo.
It looks like snow come to life, and the breath it exhales is −58 degrees Fahrenheit. Another name for it is Keokeo.
After long years in the ever-snowcapped mountains of Alola, this Vulpix has gained power over ice.
If you observe its curly hairs through a microscope, you'll see small ice particles springing up.
These Pokémon live in packs in the snowy mountains and defend themselves with icy breath. I suspect this species changed its form to adapt to the frigid climate.
They live together in a skulk, helping one another. Before eating their prey, they freeze it solid with their −58 degree Fahrenheit breath.
In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.
According to legend, a person who touched this Pokémon's tail would be cursed and become unable to feel cold for the rest of their life.
It creates beautiful auroras with its icy breath. Some believe this Pokémon is the incarnation of a mountain deity.
It can control ice and snow. People in Alola are said to have worshipped it as a deity incarnate in the blizzards of the mountain peaks.
This Pokémon has the power to create ice from the moisture in the atmosphere. The temperature in Alola isn't cold enough for it to feel at home, though.
It can produce cold air of –200 degrees Fahrenheit. It's believed to be a deity of the mountain.
This Ninetales came from a cold land. It can tell fortunes with crystal-clear accuracy when it looks into its ice crystals.
In the past, people believed that this Pokémon—Alolan Ninetales—was a holy deity that descends from the heavens to look after the mountain.
People in Alola are said to have worshipped it as a deity incarnate in the blizzards of the mountain peaks.
This Pokémon has the power to create ice from the moisture in the atmosphere. The temperature in Alola isn't cold enough for it to feel at home, though.
The three hairs on its head are made of metal. It digs through the ground using its well-developed, retractable claws.
The metal hairs on Alolan Diglett's head are a form of sensor. They're used to detect the Pokémon's surroundings.
The shiny hairs on its head are not just for show—they're actually sense organs that help it detect things both above and below ground.
An oil that promotes plant growth oozes from the tufts on its head. They cover the ground with this oil as they dig through it.
The metal whiskers on its head can detect the vibrations of others walking on the ground above it.
Because it lives underground, it's rarely seen by people. However, it leaves traces of its presence because the oil it secretes is good for plants.
The three hairs on its head are made of metal. It digs through the ground using its well-developed, retractable claws.
The metal hairs on Alolan Diglett's head are a form of sensor. They're used to detect the Pokémon's surroundings.
In Alola, their beautiful golden hair is worshipped as a gift from the gods. Some say the hair moves, but it's unclear whether this has actually been confirmed.
The golden hairs on Alolan Dugtrio's head help it sense things around it. Many Alolans see it as a symbol of good fortune.
The hair on Alolan Dugtrio is considered sacred, as it was thought to have come from a land deity. As a result, catching this Pokémon became taboo.
Long years of basking in the sun have changed Dugtrio's hair to gold. The hair is worshipped as a gift from Alola's land god.
This form of Dugtrio is worshipped as an avatar of the land gods in some places in Alola. Hunting them is strictly prohibited.
Alolans worship this Pokémon as a god. The three hairs on its head sway and shine brilliantly—a reminder of its divine origin.
Long years of basking in the sun have changed Dugtrio's hair to gold. The hair is worshipped as a gift from Alola's land god.
The golden hairs on Alolan Dugtrio's head help it sense things around it. Many Alolans see it as a symbol of good fortune.
It was a Pokémon given to a king in Alola but was unleashed when it never became useful. It adores round things, just like other Meowth.
It's possible that this Meowth changed from its normal form when it became greedy and started hoarding round objects.
If the king it served was happy, the king would rubbing the coin on Meowth's head, making the coin shine. This may have led to its current form.
This Meowth variant came about because the king who owned it lavished it with so much love and attention that it became selfish and prideful.
This form of Meowth is rarely found outside of Alola. Its coin is darker, and it has a longer face.
This Meowth has a cunning and prideful disposition. In its homeland of Alola, many people train this Pokémon.
It was a Pokémon given to a king in Alola but was unleashed when it never became useful. It adores round things, just like other Meowth.
It's possible that this Meowth changed from its normal form when it became greedy and started hoarding round objects.
In Alola, this Pokémon is adored. Its round face, its big, gentle eyes, and its plump, soft body are all worshipped as symbols of prosperity.
Possessing a somewhat grumpy character, it's popular as a status symbol among Alolan celebrities.
It has a cruel, prideful personality. It torments prey with sharp claws, then swallows the prey whole.
The Alolan Persian is admired by the people of Alola for its cool and elegant appearance. The round face is considered a symbol of beauty.
It has a round face, which is considered a mark of beauty in Alola. It taunts opponents with a look of cool, absolute disdain.
A Pokémon worshipped in Alola as a symbol of good luck. It has a cruel disposition and will torture weakened opponents.
Possessing a somewhat grumpy character, it's popular as a status symbol among Alolan celebrities.
The Alolan Persian is admired by the people of Alola for its cool and elegant appearance. The round face is considered a symbol of beauty.
Positive and negative charges generated in its body create a powerful magnetic force. It sticks to cliff faces.
Because it's so heavily magnetized, all sorts of things made of metal will stick to it. This causes it a great deal of trouble.
An electric charge is always flowing from the iron particles stuck to its body. Contact with it causes paralysis.
The strong magnetic power of the iron particles on its surface draws things to it—and sometimes it draws too many and can no longer move.
An electric charge is always flowing from the iron particles stuck to its body. Contact with it causes paralysis.
Because it's so heavily magnetized, all sorts of things made of metal will stick to it. This causes it a great deal of trouble.
In Alola, there is a form of Graveler that has iron sand stuck all over its body. It travels along cliffs, eating iron deposits.
It rolls down mountainsides, collecting iron sand as it goes. The iron sand sticks to it because of the magnetic charge in its body.
The magnetism-charged particles that cover its body can cause serious burns if touched. It's called the 'Demon of the Mountains.'
It attracts iron sand and forms iron clumps that stick to its body. This makes its skin even harder than that of Kanto Graveler.
The magnetism-charged particles that cover its body can cause serious burns if touched. It's called the 'Demon of the Mountains.'
It rolls down mountainsides, collecting iron sand as it goes. The iron sand sticks to it because of the magnetic charge in its body.
It launches electric shocks that can reach 100,000 volts. Contact with it will numb you.
It launches the rocks on its back like a railgun. Alolan Golem is hated by nearby farms, as it fires indiscriminately.
The rocks on its back have been transformed into magnetic rocks because it ate iron sand for so long. Electricity crackles between them.
A powerful Pokémon, it uses electricity to launch the rocks on its back. It's active in the mountains and creates many hazards for climbers.
The rocks on its back have been transformed into magnetic rocks because it ate iron sand for so long. Electricity crackles between them.
It launches the rocks on its back like a railgun. Alolan Golem is hated by nearby farms, as it fires indiscriminately.
Alolan Grimer came about as a way to deal with Alola's waste problem. They eat garbage thrown out by people.
When Alolan Grimer eats trash, crystallized toxins form in its body. These crystals can be colorful and quite beautiful.
It was brought to Alola to help deal with waste. It multiplied rapidly, and people have been troubled by the resulting outbreak.
The toxic crystals that emerge from its body are jewel-like and beautiful, but you must never touch them.
Alola's unique ecosystem changed it. It lives on trash, and the toxins accumulated in its body gave rise to its unique coloring.
It eats garbage and industrial waste. It's covered in crystals that solidified from the toxins and chemicals it's consumed.
It was brought to Alola to help deal with waste. It multiplied rapidly, and people have been troubled by the resulting outbreak.
The toxic crystals that emerge from its body are jewel-like and beautiful, but you must never touch them.
Its toxic body, formed by consuming nothing but poison, exudes a rainbow-colored hue. It's actually quite beautiful, but just touching it could be fatal.
What look like fangs and claws are actually crystallized poison that will afflict you at a mere touch, so don't get too close.
It appears that this Muk variant changed because of its different diet. Scientists are currently researching what effects those changes have had.
There are over a hundred kinds of poison inside its body. Chemical reactions between different poisons are the source of its vitality.
What appears to be an Alolan Muk is actually many Alolan Muk together. Their toxic bodies smear color wherever they go.
The dazzling colors of its toxic body are beautiful, but you must never touch it. Contact with it causes symptoms not unlike becoming delusional.
There are over a hundred kinds of poison inside its body. Chemical reactions between different poisons are the source of its vitality.
What look like fangs and claws are actually crystallized poison that will afflict you at a mere touch, so don't get too close.
Alola is the best environment for this Pokémon. Local people take pride in its appearance, saying this is how Exeggutor ought to look.
As it grew taller and taller, it outgrew its reliance on psychic powers, while within it awakened the power of the sleeping dragon.
Exeggutor is the pride of the Alolan people. Its image is carved into historical buildings and murals.
It swings its long neck like a whip and smacks its opponents. This makes Exeggutor itself dizzy, too.
The strong sunlight of the Alola region has awakened the power hidden within Exeggcute. This is the result.
Blazing sunlight has brought out the true form and powers of this Pokémon.
This Pokémon's psychic powers aren't as strong as they once were. The head on this Exeggutor's tail scans surrounding areas with weak telepathy.
It swings its long neck like a whip and smacks its head into opponents. This makes Exeggutor itself a little dizzy too.
As it grew taller and taller, it outgrew its reliance on psychic powers, while within it awakened the power of the sleeping dragon.
The bones it possesses were once its mother's. Its mother's regrets have become like a vengeful spirit protecting this Pokémon.
Its custom is to mourn its lost companions. Mounds of dirt by the side of the road mark the graves of the Marowak.
The rich greenery of the Alola region is hard on Marowak. It controls fire to stay alive.
When it beats opponents with its bone, the cursed flames spread to them. No amount of water will stop those flames from burning.
It has transformed the spirit of its dear departed mother into flames, and tonight it will once again dance in mourning of others of its kind.
This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.
The cursed flames that light up the bone carried by this Pokémon are said to cause both mental and physical pain that will never fade.
The rich greenery of the Alola region is hard on Marowak. It controls fire to stay alive.
When it beats opponents with its bone, the cursed flames spread to them. No amount of water will stop those flames from burning.
It has transformed the spirit of its dear departed mother into flames, and tonight it will once again dance in mourning of others of its kind.

import_notes.sql_template: INSERT OR IGNORE INTO pokemon_flavor_text (pokemon_id, game_version, flavor_text) VALUES (?, ?, ?);
import_notes.unique_constraint: UNIQUE(pokemon_id, game_version) — INSERT OR IGNORE is safe; will not overwrite any existing rows
import_notes.exeggutor_lets_go_note: Exeggutor only has a Let's Go Pikachu entry (not Let's Go Eevee) — this is correct per Bulbapedia; only one version is listed
